messages.content: How do I use `let` for Winsor McCay and Gertie in Motoi?

```motoi
;; A shy emergence: character peeks out, retreats, peeks further, emerges.
;; The retreat-and-return communicates bashfulness.

(define (bashful-emerge frame total-frames)
  (let ((progress (/ frame total-frames)))
    (cond
      ((< progress 0.15)   ; peek out
       (world/place-rock 200 300)
       (world/place-head-behind-rock 220 250 (* progress 40)))

      ((< progress 0.25)   ; retreat
       (world/place-rock 200 300)
       (world/place-head-behind-rock 220 250 (- 6 (* (- progress 0.15) 60))))

      ((< progress 0.5)    ; peek further
       (world/place-rock 200 300)
       (world/place-head-behind-rock 220 250 (* (- progress 0.25) 80)))

      ((< progress 0.6)    ; hesitate
       (world/place-rock 200 300)
       (world/place-head-behind-rock 220 250 20))

      (else                ; emerge
       (let ((x (+ 220 (* (- progress 0.6) 150))))
         (world/place-rock 200 300)
         (world/place-figure x 300 'looking-around))))))

(define (animate-emergence)
  (for-each
    (lambda (f)
      (world/reset!)
      (bashful-emerge f 48)
      (world/render!)
      (world/wait (/ 1.0 12.0)))     ; 12 fps for silent-cinema feel
    (range 0 48)))

(animate-emergence)
```

McCay's essential technique — hand-drawing each frame with careful attention to weight, anticipation, and follow-through — is what modern animators still do, just with different tools.

In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of what we can do in scheme.

```motoi
;; A literal wave — human-like motion, no exaggeration
(define (literal-wave frame total)
  (let* ((progress (/ frame total))
         (angle (* 15 (sin (* progress 2 3.14159 3)))))
    (world/place-figure 200 200 'arm-up angle)))

;; An expressive wave — exaggerated arc, held at extremes, quicker in middle
(define (expressive-wave frame total)
  (let* ((progress (/ frame total))
         (t (* progress 2 3.14159 3))
         ;; Exaggerate amplitude
         (amp 30)
         ;; Add slight hold at extremes with a squared sine
         (angle (* amp (* (sin t) (abs (sin t))))))
    (world/place-figure 200 200 'arm-up angle)))

(define (compare-waves)
  (for-each
    (lambda (f)
      (world/reset!)
      (literal-wave f 48)
      (world/render! 'panel-a)
      (world/reset!)
      (expressive-wave f 48)
      (world/render! 'panel-b)
      (world/wait (/ 1.0 24.0)))
    (range 0 48)))

(compare-waves)
```

We can simulate the difference between literal-motion animation and expressive-motion animation. Both animate the same basic gesture (a wave), but with different curvature and timing.

```motoi
;; A three-beat comic timing: setup, response, sting.
;; Each beat is a specific duration measured in frames at 24 fps.

(define (setup-beat frame)
  ;; Character enters, notices something. Duration: 24 frames (1 sec)
  (world/place-character 200 200 'noticing))

(define (response-beat frame)
  ;; Character reacts subtly. Duration: 18 frames (0.75 sec)
  (world/place-character 200 200 'about-to-react))

(define (sting-beat frame)
  ;; Character takes an extreme action. Duration: 6 frames (0.25 sec)
  (world/place-character 200 200 'extreme-reaction))

(define (comic-timing-scene)
  (transport/fps 24)
  ;; Setup: 24 frames
  (for-each (lambda (f) (setup-beat f) (world/step)) (range 0 24))
  ;; Response: 18 frames
  (for-each (lambda (f) (response-beat f) (world/step)) (range 0 18))
  ;; Sting: 6 frames
  (for-each (lambda (f) (sting-beat f) (world/step)) (range 0 6)))
```

The Termite Terrace comic-timing pattern can be captured in a small program.

How do I use `let` for UPA modernism in Motoi?

```motoi
;; UPA-style scene: a character speaks. Only the mouth animates.
;; The body is a graphic design in a colored area.

(define (upa-character-still)
  ;; Draw once and hold
  (world/background 'salmon)                           ; flat colored background
  (world/place-shape 'rectangle 100 200 200 300 'olive)  ; simple furniture
  (world/place-figure 'stylized 250 200 'stiff-body)   ; angular figure
  (world/set-eye-position 250 220 'looking-right))

(define (animate-mouth frame)
  ;; The only animation: the mouth opening and closing
  (let ((mouth-open? (odd? (quotient frame 6))))
    (if mouth-open?
        (world/set-mouth 250 240 'open)
        (world/set-mouth 250 240 'closed))))

(define (upa-scene)
  ;; Draw the character once. Hold. Animate only the mouth.
  (upa-character-still)
  (for-each
    (lambda (f)
      (animate-mouth f)
      (world/render!)
      (world/wait (/ 1.0 24.0)))
    (range 0 96)))    ; four seconds
```

Let's build a small UPA-style scene: limited animation, flat background, held poses.

How do I work with characters for Ghibli and hand-drawn features in Motoi?

```motoi
;; A slow-paced observation scene, Ghibli-style.
;; The character walks, then stops, then looks at the environment.
;; Pacing: 24 frames per second, but the scene runs for a full 8 seconds.

(define (field-background t)
  (world/background 'pale-sky-blue)
  (world/place-shape 'gradient 0 300 800 400 'meadow-green)
  ;; Grass that ripples with the wind, animated by t
  (let ((wind-offset (* 3 (sin (* t 0.5)))))
    (world/place-grass 100 350 700 380 wind-offset)))

(define (walking-frame t)
  (let ((x (+ 100 (* 40 t))))    ; slow forward motion
    (world/place-character x 320 'walking-quietly)))

(define (paused-frame t)
  (world/place-character 260 320 'still-looking))

(define (wind-watching-frame t)
  ;; Character is still, but her hair moves in the wind
  (let ((hair-offset (* 5 (sin (* t 1.0)))))
    (world/place-character 260 320 'still-looking hair-offset)))

(define (ghibli-scene)
  (transport/fps 24)
  ;; Seconds 0-3: character walks
  (let loop ((t 0))
    (when (< t 3.0)
      (world/reset!)
      (field-background t)
      (walking-frame t)
      (world/render!)
      (world/wait (/ 1.0 24.0))
      (loop (+ t (/ 1.0 24.0)))))

  ;; Seconds 3-4: character stops
  (let loop ((t 3.0))
    (when (< t 4.0)
      (world/reset!)
      (field-background t)
      (paused-frame t)
      (world/render!)
      (world/wait (/ 1.0 24.0))
      (loop (+ t (/ 1.0 24.0)))))

  ;; Seconds 4-8: character watches the wind (four seconds)
  (let loop ((t 4.0))
    (when (< t 8.0)
      (world/reset!)
      (field-background t)
      (wind-watching-frame t)
      (world/render!)
      (world/wait (/ 1.0 24.0))
      (loop (+ t (/ 1.0 24.0))))))
```

A small Ghibli-inflected scene: a character walks through a field, pauses, watches the wind.

How do I work with Satoshi Kon in Motoi?

```motoi
;; Two scenes that share the same visual composition but different contexts.
;; The cut between them uses the same pose to disorient the audience.

(define (scene-a-street-walk t)
  ;; Character walks down a rainy Tokyo street at night
  (world/background 'dark-blue)
  (world/place-rain)
  (world/place-street-signs)
  (let ((x (+ 100 (* t 30))))
    (world/place-character x 320 'walking 'blue-umbrella)))

(define (scene-b-dream-street t)
  ;; Same character, same pose — but the street is now surreal
  (world/background 'pink-cotton-candy)
  (world/place-floating-fish)
  (world/place-clock-with-melted-face)
  (let ((x (+ 100 (* t 30))))
    (world/place-character x 320 'walking 'blue-umbrella)))

(define (kon-cut-scene)
  ;; Scene A: three seconds of street walk
  (let loop ((t 0))
    (when (< t 3.0)
      (world/reset!)
      (scene-a-street-walk t)
      (world/render!)
      (world/wait (/ 1.0 24.0))
      (loop (+ t (/ 1.0 24.0)))))

  ;; The cut — exactly matched frame from Scene A to Scene B.
  ;; The character's pose is IDENTICAL across the cut.
  ;; Only the environment changes.

  ;; Scene B: three more seconds — but now in a dream
  (let loop ((t 3.0))
    (when (< t 6.0)
      (world/reset!)
      (scene-b-dream-street t)
      (world/render!)
      (world/wait (/ 1.0 24.0))
      (loop (+ t (/ 1.0 24.0))))))
```

A small demonstration of a Kon-style matched cut.

How do I work with Norman McLaren in Motoi?

```motoi
;; Given a sequence of frames, produce a new sequence where each frame is
;; overlaid with the frames a few positions before and after.

(define (overlaid-frame all-frames index)
  (let* ((n (length all-frames))
         (indices (list (max 0 (- index 6))
                        (max 0 (- index 3))
                        index
                        (min (- n 1) (+ index 3))
                        (min (- n 1) (+ index 6))))
         (frames (map (lambda (i) (list-ref all-frames i)) indices))
         (alphas '(0.2 0.4 1.0 0.4 0.2)))
    (world/reset!)
    (for-each
      (lambda (frame alpha)
        (world/place-frame frame :alpha alpha))
      frames alphas)
    (world/render!)))

(define (overlaid-time-animation frames)
  (transport/fps 24)
  (for-each
    (lambda (i)
      (overlaid-frame frames i)
      (world/wait (/ 1.0 24.0)))
    (range 0 (length frames))))
```

A small demonstration of overlaid-time animation (the *Pas de deux* trick).

In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of what we can do in scheme.

```motoi
;; Stop-motion animation typically runs at 12 or 24 fps but each frame is a
;; discrete, held pose — no in-between motion blur. We can simulate this by
;; using stepped positions rather than continuous motion.

(define (stop-motion-step t total-time discrete-frames)
  ;; Given continuous time t, snap it to one of the discrete frames.
  (let* ((frame-duration (/ total-time discrete-frames))
         (frame-index (floor (/ t frame-duration))))
    (/ frame-index discrete-frames)))

(define (stop-motion-walk duration)
  (let ((discrete-frames 12))    ; 12 discrete poses across the walk
    (let loop ((t 0))
      (when (< t duration)
        (let* ((discrete-t (stop-motion-step t duration discrete-frames))
               (x (+ 100 (* discrete-t 400))))
          (world/reset!)
          (world/place-puppet x 300 'walking-pose (floor (* discrete-t 12)))
          (world/render!)
          (world/wait (/ 1.0 24.0))
          (loop (+ t (/ 1.0 24.0))))))))

(stop-motion-walk 3.0)
```

We cannot really do stop-motion in Scheme — the technique requires physical objects and cameras. But we can simulate its rhythmic characteristics.

How do I use symbols for spawning an entity in Motoi?

```motoi
;; Spawn a single entity at the origin.
(world/reset!)
(world/spawn 'coin '((x . 400) (y . 300) (sprite . "coin")))
(world/step)
```

An entity is a thing in the world: a coin, a leaf, a character. We bring one into existence with `world/spawn`, giving it a name tag (a symbol) and initial properties.

```motoi
;; Spawn a coin and read its position.
(world/reset!)
(define coin (world/spawn 'coin '((x . 400) (y . 300) (sprite . "coin"))))
(world/step)

(entity/pos coin)  ;; => (400 . 300)
(entity/x coin)    ;; => 400
(entity/y coin)    ;; => 300
```

```motoi
;; Spawn five coins in a horizontal line using a loop.
(world/reset!)
(let loop ((i 0))
  (when (< i 5)
    (let* ((x (+ 100 (* i 150)))
           (y 300)
           (name (string->symbol (string-append "coin-" (number->string i)))))
      (world/spawn name `((x . ,x) (y . ,y) (sprite . "coin"))))
    (loop (+ i 1))))
(world/step)
```

In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of circle placement.

```motoi
;; Spawn eight coins in a circle.
(world/reset!)
(let* ((cx 400)
       (cy 300)
       (radius 120))
  (let loop ((i 0))
    (when (< i 8)
      (let* ((angle (* (/ i 8.0) TAU))
             (x (+ cx (* radius (dcos angle))))
             (y (+ cy (* radius (dsin angle))))
             (name (string->symbol (string-append "coin-" (number->string i)))))
        (world/spawn name `((x . ,x) (y . ,y) (sprite . "coin"))))
      (loop (+ i 1)))))
(world/step)
```

```motoi
;; Spawn a 5×3 grid of coins.
(world/reset!)
(let ((x-start 150)
      (y-start 150)
      (x-spacing 120)
      (y-spacing 120))
  (let row-loop ((row 0))
    (when (< row 3)
      (let col-loop ((col 0))
        (when (< col 5)
          (let* ((x (+ x-start (* col x-spacing)))
                 (y (+ y-start (* row y-spacing)))
                 (name (string->symbol (string-append "coin-" (number->string row) "-" (number->string col)))))
            (world/spawn name `((x . ,x) (y . ,y) (sprite . "coin"))))
          (col-loop (+ col 1))))
      (row-loop (+ row 1)))))
(world/step)
```

```motoi
;; Spawn coins along the four edges of the screen.
(world/reset!)
(let ((margin 50)
      (spacing 120))
  ;; Top edge
  (let loop ((i 0))
    (when (< i 6)
      (let ((x (+ 100 (* i spacing)))
            (y margin))
        (world/spawn (string->symbol (string-append "top-" (number->string i)))
                     `((x . ,x) (y . ,y) (sprite . "coin"))))
      (loop (+ i 1))))
  ;; Bottom edge
  (let loop ((i 0))
    (when (< i 6)
      (let ((x (+ 100 (* i spacing)))
            (y (- 600 margin)))
        (world/spawn (string->symbol (string-append "bottom-" (number->string i)))
                     `((x . ,x) (y . ,y) (sprite . "coin"))))
      (loop (+ i 1))))
  ;; Left edge
  (let loop ((i 0))
    (when (< i 3)
      (let ((x margin)
            (y (+ 150 (* i 120))))
        (world/spawn (string->symbol (string-append "left-" (number->string i)))
                     `((x . ,x) (y . ,y) (sprite . "coin"))))
      (loop (+ i 1))))
  ;; Right edge
  (let loop ((i 0))
    (when (< i 3)
      (let ((x (- 800 margin))
            (y (+ 150 (* i 120))))
        (world/spawn (string->symbol (string-append "right-" (number->string i)))
                     `((x . ,x) (y . ,y) (sprite . "coin"))))
      (loop (+ i 1)))))
(world/step)
```

```motoi
;; Spawn a central coin, then four satellites around it.

(define center (world/spawn 'center '((x . 400) (y . 300) (sprite . "coin"))))

      (cy (entity/y center))
      (orbit-radius 80))
  (let loop ((i 0))
    (when (< i 4)
      (let* ((angle (* (/ i 4.0) TAU))
             (x (+ cx (* orbit-radius (dcos angle))))
             (y (+ cy (* orbit-radius (dsin angle))))
             (name (string->symbol (string-append "satellite-" (number->string i)))))
        (world/spawn name `((x . ,x) (y . ,y) (sprite . "coin"))))
      (loop (+ i 1)))))
(world/step)
```

In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of staggered rows.

```motoi
;; Spawn coins in a staggered grid.
(world/reset!)
(let ((x-spacing 100)
      (y-spacing 80)
      (rows 4)
      (cols 6))
  (let row-loop ((row 0))
    (when (< row rows)
      (let* ((row-offset (if (= (modulo row 2) 0) 0 50))
             (col-limit (if (= (modulo row 2) 0) cols (- cols 1))))
        (let col-loop ((col 0))
          (when (< col col-limit)
            (let* ((x (+ 100 row-offset (* col x-spacing)))
                   (y (+ 100 (* row y-spacing)))
                   (name (string->symbol (string-append "coin-" (number->string row) "-" (number->string col)))))
              (world/spawn name `((x . ,x) (y . ,y) (sprite . "coin"))))
            (col-loop (+ col 1)))))
      (row-loop (+ row 1)))))
(world/step)
```

```motoi
;; Spawn coins in three concentric circles.

(world/step)
```

```motoi
;; Spawn coins approximating a Sierpiński triangle (3 levels).
(world/reset!)
(define (draw-triangle cx cy size level)
  (when (> level 0)
    (let* ((half-size (/ size 2))
           (h (* size 0.866)))
      (world/spawn (string->symbol (string-append "t-" (number->string level) "-" (number->string cx) "-" (number->string cy)))
                   `((x . ,cx) (y . ,cy) (sprite . "coin")))
      (draw-triangle cx (- cy (/ h 2)) half-size (- level 1))
      (draw-triangle (- cx (/ size 4)) (+ cy (/ h 4)) half-size (- level 1))
      (draw-triangle (+ cx (/ size 4)) (+ cy (/ h 4)) half-size (- level 1)))))

(draw-triangle 400 300 200 3)
(world/step)
```

```motoi
;; Spawn coins in a checkerboard pattern.

      (cols 8)
      (cell-size 60)
      (x-start 100)
      (y-start 100))
  (let row-loop ((r 0))
    (when (< r rows)
      (let col-loop ((c 0))
        (when (< c cols)
          (when (= (modulo (+ r c) 2) 0)
            (let ((x (+ x-start (* c cell-size) (/ cell-size 2)))

          (col-loop (+ c 1))))
      (row-loop (+ r 1)))))
(world/step)
```

```motoi
      (ray-loop (+ ray 1)))))
```

How do I work with collision-free placement (simulated) in Motoi?

```motoi
;; Spawn coins ensuring no two are closer than a threshold.
(world/reset!)
(define min-separation 80)
(define candidates '())

(define (too-close? x y)
  (let loop ((cs candidates))
    (if (null? cs)
        #f
        (let* ((c (car cs))
               (cx (car c))
               (cy (cdr c))
               (dx (- x cx))
               (dy (- y cy))
               (dist (dsqrt (+ (* dx dx) (* dy dy)))))
          (if (< dist min-separation)
              #t
              (loop (cdr cs)))))))

(let attempt-loop ((i 0) (placed 0))
  (when (and (< placed 15) (< i 100))
    (let* ((seed-x (+ 8000 (* i 97)))
           (seed-y (+ 9000 (* i 131)))
           (x (+ 100 (* 600 (- (dsin seed-x) -1) 0.5)))
           (y (+ 100 (* 400 (- (dsin seed-y) -1) 0.5))))
      (if (too-close? x y)
          (attempt-loop (+ i 1) placed)
          (begin
            (set! candidates (cons (cons x y) candidates))
            (world/spawn (string->symbol (string-append "coin-" (number->string placed)))
                         `((x . ,x) (y . ,y) (sprite . "coin")))
            (attempt-loop (+ i 1) (+ placed 1)))))))

(world/step)
```

```motoi
    (loop (+ i 1))))
(world/step)

(world/count)  ;; => 7
```
How many entities are in the world?

```motoi
;; Spawn coins, then despawn any with x > 500.
(world/reset!)
(world/spawn 'coin-a '((x . 200) (y . 300) (sprite . "coin")))
(world/spawn 'coin-b '((x . 400) (y . 300) (sprite . "coin")))
(world/spawn 'coin-c '((x . 600) (y . 300) (sprite . "coin")))
(world/spawn 'coin-d '((x . 700) (y . 300) (sprite . "coin")))
(world/step)

(world/each
```

```motoi
;; Spawn coins, take a snapshot, change the world, restore.

(define coin-a (world/spawn 'coin-a '((x . 200) (y . 300) (sprite . "coin"))))

(world/step)

(world/restore! snapshot)
```

Show me a recursive example of grid in Motoi.

```motoi
(define (row x y n)
  (if (zero? n)
      '()
      (cons (entity 'circle (at x y) (radius 5) (fill 'slateblue))
            (row (+ x 20) y (- n 1)))))

(define (grid x y cols rows)
  (if (zero? rows)
      '()
      (append (row x y cols)
              (grid x (+ y 20) cols (- rows 1)))))

(define (frame t)
  (apply world (grid 100 80 10 6)))

(render-at 0.0)
```

Show me a recursive example of recursive checkerboard in Motoi.

```motoi
(define (row x y n color1 color2)
  (if (zero? n)
      '()
      (cons (entity 'rect (at x y) (width 25) (height 25) (fill color1))
            (row (+ x 25) y (- n 1) color2 color1))))

(define (board x y cols rows)
  (if (zero? rows)
      '()
      (append (row x y cols (if (even? rows) 'black 'white)
                            (if (even? rows) 'white 'black))
              (board x (+ y 25) cols (- rows 1)))))

(define (frame t)
  (apply world
         (cons (background 'beige)
               (board 80 80 8 8))))

(render-at 0.0)
```

In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of orbits (a preview).

```motoi
(world/reset!)
(define moon (world/spawn 'moon 3 0))
(let loop ((n 120))
  (when (> n 0)
    (let* ((pos (entity/pos moon))
           (x (car pos))
           (y (cadr pos))
           (angle (datan2 y x))
           (speed 2))
      (entity/set-vel! moon (* speed (- (dsin angle))) (* speed (dcos angle)))
      (world/step 0.016)
      (loop (- n 1)))))
(entity/pos moon)
```

If you change velocity *every frame* to always point perpendicular to the current position (relative to a center), the entity orbits. This is a sneak peek—Chapter 8 will drill oscillation thoroughly—but it shows how velocity can be dynamic.

How do I work with lists for chase (constant velocity toward a target) in Motoi?

```motoi
(world/reset!)
(define sakura (world/spawn 'sakura 5 5))
(define cat (world/spawn 'cat -3 -2))
(entity/set-vel! sakura 0 0)
(let ((chase-speed 3))
  (let loop ((n 60))
    (when (> n 0)
      (let* ((cat-pos (entity/pos cat))
             (sakura-pos (entity/pos sakura))
             (dx (- (car sakura-pos) (car cat-pos)))
             (dy (- (cadr sakura-pos) (cadr cat-pos)))
             (dist (dsqrt (+ (* dx dx) (* dy dy)))))
        (when (> dist 0.1)
          (entity/set-vel! cat 
                           (* chase-speed (/ dx dist)) 
                           (* chase-speed (/ dy dist))))
        (world/step 0.016)
        (loop (- n 1)))))
(entity/pos cat))
```

How do I work with flocking separation (avoid crowding) in Motoi?

```motoi
(world/reset!)
(define a (world/spawn 'bird 0 0))
(define b (world/spawn 'bird 0.3 0.2))
(define c (world/spawn 'bird -0.2 0.1))
(entity/set-vel! a 1 0)
(entity/set-vel! b 1 0)
(entity/set-vel! c 1 0)
(let loop ((n 60))
  (when (> n 0)
    (for-each
     (lambda (self)
       (let ((sep-x 0) (sep-y 0))
         (for-each
          (lambda (other)
            (when (not (eq? self other))
              (let* ((self-pos (entity/pos self))
                     (other-pos (entity/pos other))
                     (dx (- (car self-pos) (car other-pos)))
                     (dy (- (cadr self-pos) (cadr other-pos)))
                     (dist (dsqrt (+ (* dx dx) (* dy dy)))))
                (when (< dist 1.0)
                  (set! sep-x (+ sep-x dx))
                  (set! sep-y (+ sep-y dy))))))
          (list a b c))
         (let ((v (entity/vel self)))
           (entity/set-vel! self (+ (car v) (* 0.1 sep-x)) (+ (cadr v) (* 0.1 sep-y))))))
     (list a b c))
    (world/step 0.016)
    (loop (- n 1))))
(map entity/pos (list a b c))
```

```motoi
(world/reset!)
```

```motoi
  (entity/set-vel! bullet (+ (car ship-vel) 5) (+ (cadr ship-vel) 0))
  (let loop ((n 40))
    (when (> n 0)
      (world/step 0.016)
      (loop (- n 1))))
  (list (entity/pos ship) (entity/pos bullet)))
```
Spawn a projectile from a moving entity. Give the projectile the parent's current velocity *plus* a firing velocity.

```motoi
    (world/step 0.016)
    (loop (- n 1))))
```

In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of following a piecewise path.

```motoi
(define (route t)
  (let* ((segment-duration 1.0)
         (phase (modulo t (* 2 segment-duration)))
         (v 100)
         (x (if (< phase segment-duration)
                (* phase v)
                v))
         (y (if (< phase segment-duration)
                0
                (* (- phase segment-duration) v))))
    (world
      (entity 'corner-turner
              (placement x y)
              (shapes
                (rect -8 -8 16 16 '#x16A085))))))
```

In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of bouncing between walls (continuous model).

```motoi
(define (route t)
  (let* ((period 3.0)
         (phase (modulo t period))
         (v 160)
         (x-left 40)
         (x-right 440)
         (x-range (- x-right x-left))
         (raw-x (* phase v))
         (bounces (floor (/ raw-x x-range)))
         (x-in-range (modulo raw-x x-range))
         (x (if (even? bounces)
                (+ x-left x-in-range)
                (- x-right x-in-range))))
    (world
      (entity 'bouncer
              (placement x 240)
              (shapes
                (circle 0 0 10 '#xF4D03F))))))
```

How do I `define` custom easing via interpolation factor in Motoi?

```motoi
(let ((box (world/spawn 'box :x 50 :y 300))
      (start-x 50)
      (end-x 350)
      (frames 60))
  (letrec ((ease-out (lambda (t) (- 1 (* (- 1 t) (- 1 t)))))
           (step (lambda (frame)
                   (when (< frame frames)
                     (let* ((t (/ frame frames))
                            (eased-t (ease-out t))
                            (x (+ start-x (* (- end-x start-x) eased-t))))
                       (entity/set-pos! box x 300)
                       (world/step 1)
                       (step (+ frame 1)))))))
    (step 0)))
```

In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of mirrored ease-in from ease-out.

```motoi
(let ((box (world/spawn 'box :x 50 :y 300))
      (start-x 50)
      (end-x 350)
      (frames 60))
  (letrec ((ease-out (lambda (t) (- 1 (* (- 1 t) (- 1 t)))))
           (ease-in (lambda (t) (- 1 (ease-out (- 1 t)))))
           (step (lambda (frame)
                   (when (< frame frames)
                     (let* ((t (/ frame frames))
                            (eased-t (ease-in t))
                            (x (+ start-x (* (- end-x start-x) eased-t))))
                       (entity/set-pos! box x 300)
                       (world/step 1)
                       (step (+ frame 1)))))))
```

```motoi
                  (else (let ((t2 (/ (- t 2.0) 1.0)))
                          (+ 0.666 (* (- 1 (* (- 1 t2) (- 1 t2))) 0.333))))))
         (x (+ 100 (* eased 600))))
    (fill 100 250 200)
    (circle x 350 28)))
```

A circle eases in for 1 second, moves linearly for 1 second, eases out for 1 second.

```motoi
  (entity/sprite! m2 'circle 5 '(255 180 120))
  (entity/sprite! m3 'circle 3 '(255 220 110))
  (entity/sprite! m4 'circle 6 '(255 190 100))
  (motion/arc m1 :dx 300 :apex -180 :frames 80 :ease 'linear)
  (motion/arc m2 :dx 450 :apex -220 :frames 100 :ease 'linear)
  (motion/arc m3 :dx 250 :apex -160 :frames 70 :ease 'linear)
  (motion/arc m4 :dx 380 :apex -200 :frames 90 :ease 'linear)
  (world/step 200))
```

Multiple meteors arc across the sky, each with a different trajectory.

In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of asymmetric timing.

```motoi
;; Fast rise, slow fall
(define (ease-in-cubic t)
  (* t t t))

(define (ease-out-cubic t)
  (let ((t1 (- t 1)))
    (+ 1 (* t1 t1 t1))))

(define (draw)
  (let* ((raw-t (/ (mod (millis) 3000) 3000.0))
         (t (if (< raw-t 0.3)
                (/ raw-t 0.3)
                (/ (- raw-t 0.3) 0.7)))
         (ease-fn (if (< raw-t 0.3) ease-in-cubic ease-out-cubic))
         (x (+ 60 (* raw-t 280)))
         (y (+ 250 (* -180 (ease-fn t)))))
    (motion/clear)
    (motion/circle x y 11 "fill")))

(draw)
```

The rise and fall don't have to take the same amount of time.

```motoi
;; Reaching gesture with ease-in-out on both axes

      (* 4 t t t)
      (let ((t2 (- (* 2 t) 2)))
        (+ 1 (* 0.5 t2 t2 t2)))))

  (let* ((t (ease-in-out-cubic (/ (mod (millis) 2500) 2500.0)))
         (x0 80) (y0 320)
         (x1 280) (y1 160)
         (control-y (- y0 60))
         (x (+ x0 (* (- x1 x0) t)))
         (y (+ y0 (* (- control-y y0) (sin (* t 3.14159)))))
         (y-final (+ y (* (- y1 y) t))))
    (motion/clear)
    ;; Start and end markers
    (motion/circle x0 y0 6 "stroke")
    (motion/circle x1 y1 6 "stroke")
    ;; Hand position
    (motion/circle x y-final 12 "fill")))

(draw)
```

```motoi
;; Arc with trailing positions
(define (draw)
  (let* ((base-t (/ (mod (millis) 2500) 2500.0)))
    (motion/clear)
    ;; Draw trail
    (let loop ((i 0))
      (when (< i 8)
        (let* ((offset (* i 0.03))
               (t (max 0 (- base-t offset)))
               (x (+ 60 (* t 280)))
               (y (+ 250 (* -140 (sin (* t 3.14159)))))
               (alpha (- 1.0 (* i 0.12))))
          (motion/circle x y 8 "fill"))
        (loop (+ i 1))))
    ;; Draw main dot
    (let* ((x (+ 60 (* base-t 280)))
           (y (+ 250 (* -140 (sin (* base-t 3.14159))))))
      (motion/circle x y 12 "fill"))))

(draw)
```

```motoi
;; Pause at apex
(define (ease-pause-middle t)
  (cond
    ((< t 0.4) (* 2.5 t))
    ((< t 0.6) 1.0)
    (else (+ 1.0 (* 2.5 (- t 0.6))))))

(define (draw)
  (let* ((raw-t (/ (mod (millis) 3000) 3000.0))
         (t (min 2.0 (ease-pause-middle raw-t)))
         (t-clamped (min 1.0 t))
         (x (+ 60 (* t-clamped 280)))
         (y (+ 260 (* -150 (sin (* t-clamped 3.14159))))))
    (motion/clear)
    (motion/circle x y 11 "fill")))

(draw)
```

The motion slows to a stop at the highest point, then resumes.

```motoi
         (x3 340) (y3 300)
         (t1 (- 1 t))
         (x (+ (* t1 t1 t1 x0)

               (* 3 t1 t t x2)
               (* t t t x3)))
         (y (+ (* t1 t1 t1 y0)
               (* 3 t1 t1 t y1)
               (* 3 t1 t t y2)
               (* t t t y3))))
    (motion/clear)
    ;; Draw control points
    (motion/circle x0 y0 5 "stroke")
    (motion/circle x1 y1 5 "stroke")
    (motion/circle x2 y2 5 "stroke")
    (motion/circle x3 y3 5 "stroke")
    ;; Draw the moving point
    (motion/circle x y 11 "fill")))

(draw)
```

```motoi
;; Whip motion: slow start, explosive middle, quick settle
(define (ease-whip t)
  (cond
    ((< t 0.2) (* 1.25 t t))
    ((< t 0.4) (+ 0.05 (* 12 (- t 0.2) (- t 0.2))))
    (else (+ 1.0 (* -5 (- t 0.4) (- t 0.4))))))

(define (draw)
  (let* ((t (/ (mod (millis) 1400) 1400.0))
         (x (+ 80 (* t 240)))
         (y (+ 220 (* -180 (ease-whip t)))))
    (motion/clear)
    (motion/circle x y 10 "fill")))

(draw)
```

```motoi
(let ((dots (list (world/spawn 'dot 100 100)
                  (world/spawn 'dot 100 130)
                  (world/spawn 'dot 100 160)
```

How do I sign timing a delayed reaction in Motoi?

```motoi
;; Signal and delayed response
(let ((w (world/make 400 300)))
  (world/add w 'signal (entity/make 'circle 100 150 15))
  (world/add w 'response (entity/make 'rect 300 125 30 30))
  (entity/fill w 'signal "#e63946")
  (entity/fill w 'response "#a8dadc")
  ;; Signal pulses (grows)
  (motion/ease w 'signal 'radius 25 0.3 'quad-out)
  ;; Response starts 1 second later
  (world/after w 1.0 (lambda () (motion/ease w 'response 'y 200 0.6 'quad-out)))
  (world/render w 0.0)
  (world/render w 0.3)
  (world/render w 1.0)
  (world/render w 1.6))
```

```motoi
  (motion/ease w 'heart 'height 70 0.2 'quad-out)
  (world/after w 0.2 (lambda ()
    (motion/ease w 'heart 'width 50 0.2 'quad-in)
    (motion/ease w 'heart 'height 50 0.2 'quad-in)))
  ;; Second beat
  (world/after w 0.6 (lambda ()
    (motion/ease w 'heart 'width 70 0.2 'quad-out)
    (motion/ease w 'heart 'height 70 0.2 'quad-out)))
  (world/after w 0.8 (lambda ()
    (motion/ease w 'heart 'width 50 0.2 'quad-in)
    (motion/ease w 'heart 'height 50 0.2 'quad-in)))
  ;; Third beat
  (world/after w 1.2 (lambda ()
    (motion/ease w 'heart 'width 70 0.2 'quad-out)
    (motion/ease w 'heart 'height 70 0.2 'quad-out)))
  (world/after w 1.4 (lambda ()
    (motion/ease w 'heart 'width 50 0.2 'quad-in)
    (motion/ease w 'heart 'height 50 0.2 'quad-in)))
  (world/render w 0.0)
  (world/render w 0.6)
  (world/render w 1.2)
  (world/render w 1.8))
```

```motoi
;; Domino chain

  (world/render w 0.0)
  (world/render w 0.2)
  (world/render w 0.4)
  (world/render w 0.8))
```

In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of timing a crescendo.

```motoi
;; Accelerating tempo
(let ((w (world/make 500 200)))
  (world/add w 'a (entity/make 'circle 50 100 12))
  (world/add w 'b (entity/make 'circle 50 100 12))
  (world/add w 'c (entity/make 'circle 50 100 12))
  (world/add w 'd (entity/make 'circle 50 100 12))
  (world/add w 'e (entity/make 'circle 50 100 12))
  (entity/fill w 'a "#f94144")
  (entity/fill w 'b "#f3722c")
  (entity/fill w 'c "#f8961e")
  (entity/fill w 'd "#f9c74f")
  (entity/fill w 'e "#90be6d")
  (world/after w 0.0 (lambda () (motion/ease w 'a 'x 100 0.3 'linear)))
  (world/after w 1.0 (lambda () (motion/ease w 'b 'x 180 0.3 'linear)))
  (world/after w 1.8 (lambda () (motion/ease w 'c 'x 260 0.3 'linear)))
  (world/after w 2.4 (lambda () (motion/ease w 'd 'x 340 0.3 'linear)))
  (world/after w 2.8 (lambda () (motion/ease w 'e 'x 420 0.3 'linear)))
  (world/render w 0.0)
  (world/render w 1.0)
  (world/render w 2.0)
  (world/render w 3.1))
```

Five events happen, each slightly faster than the previous. The accelerating tempo creates a crescendo.

In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of composite timing: a multi-act scene.

```motoi
;; Six-act timing composition
(let ((w (world/make 500 300)))
  (world/add w 'actor (entity/make 'circle 0 150 20))
  (entity/fill w 'actor "#9d4edd")
  ;; Act 1: Silence (nothing)
  ;; Act 2: Entrance (1.0-2.0s)
  (world/after w 1.0 (lambda () (motion/ease w 'actor 'x 150 1.0 'quad-out)))
  ;; Act 3: Pause (2.0-2.8s)
  ;; Act 4: Gesture (2.8-3.4s)
  (world/after w 2.8 (lambda () (motion/ease w 'actor 'y 100 0.3 'quad-out)))
  (world/after w 3.1 (lambda () (motion/ease w 'actor 'y 150 0.3 'quad-in)))
  ;; Act 5: Pause (3.4-4.0s)
  ;; Act 6: Exit (4.0-4.8s)
  (world/after w 4.0 (lambda () (motion/ease w 'actor 'x 500 0.8 'quad-in)))
  (world/render w 0.0)
  (world/render w 2.0)
  (world/render w 3.1)
  (world/render w 4.8))
```

A complete mini-scene composed purely through timing: silence, entrance, pause, gesture, pause, exit.

In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of the falling leaf: oscillation and drift.

```motoi
(world/reset!)
(world/floor! 100)

(let ((leaf (world/spawn 'leaf :x 150 :y 300 :color "#7CFC00")))
  (entity/gravity-scale! leaf 0.25)
  (entity/drag! leaf 0.75)
  (entity/bounce! leaf 0.0)
  (entity/mass! leaf 0.1)
  (letrec ((sway (lambda (t)
                   (when (< t 300)
                     (entity/set-vel! leaf (* 15 (dsin (* t 0.1))) 0)
                     (world/step 1)
                     (sway (+ t 1))))))
    (sway 0)))
```

A leaf doesn't fall straight. It **tumbles**, swaying side to side as drag and turbulence nudge it.

In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of the dropped chain: segmented settle.

```motoi
(world/reset!)
(world/floor! 100)

(let ((links (map (lambda (i)
                    (world/spawn 'circle :x (+ 140 (* i 5)) :y (+ 250 (* i 3)) :color "#C0C0C0"))
                  (list 0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7))))
  (for-each (lambda (link)
              (entity/gravity-scale! link 1.2)
              (entity/drag! link 0.1)
              (entity/bounce! link 0.2)
              (entity/mass! link 0.5)
              (entity/friction! link 0.6))
            links)
  (world/step 180))
```

A chain doesn't settle as one piece — each link lands and settles independently, creating a **cascade** of micro-impacts.

In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of overshoot and settle.

```motoi
(motion
  (beat 80)
  (let* ((t (frame))
         (target 300)
         (damping 0.92)
         (spring-k 0.05)
         (initial-pos 100))
    (define pos initial-pos)
    (define vel 0)
    (set! vel (+ vel (* spring-k (- target pos))))
    (set! vel (* vel damping))
    (set! pos (+ pos vel))
    (entity weight
      (pos pos 200)
      (radius 18)
      (color 80 80 80))
    (until (> t 300))))
```

Start with the simplest case: a circle at rest that we can squash and stretch by scaling along its local axes. We won't move it yet — just reshape it.
In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of squash on impact, stretch on release.

```motoi
;; A ball that squashes when it lands, then springs back
(let ((ball (world/spawn :kind 'circle :x 200 :y 50 :radius 24 :vy 0)))
  (entity/gravity-scale! ball 1.0)
  (entity/bounce! ball 0.8)
  (entity/set! ball 'floor 300)
  
  (world/after 0 (lambda ()
    (world/each (lambda (e)
      (let* ((ground 300)
             (dist-to-floor (- ground (entity/y e)))
             (squash-amount (if (< dist-to-floor 5) 1.5 1.0))
             (stretch-amount (if (< dist-to-floor 5) 0.6 1.0)))
        (entity/set! e 'scale-x squash-amount)
        (entity/set! e 'scale-y stretch-amount)))))))
```

A bounce has two moments when deformation is strongest: when the ball *hits* the ground (squash) and when it *leaves* the ground (stretch). Between those moments, the ball tends back toward its rest shape.

What does this Motoi Scheme program do?

```motoi
;; A ball that squashes when it lands, then springs back
(let ((ball (world/spawn :kind 'circle :x 200 :y 50 :radius 24 :vy 0)))
  (entity/gravity-scale! ball 1.0)
  (entity/bounce! ball 0.8)
  (entity/set! ball 'floor 300)
  
  (world/after 0 (lambda ()
    (world/each (lambda (e)
      (let* ((ground 300)
             (dist-to-floor (- ground (entity/y e)))
             (squash-amount (if (< dist-to-floor 5) 1.5 1.0))

```

A bounce has two moments when deformation is strongest: when the ball *hits* the ground (squash) and when it *leaves* the ground (stretch). Between those moments, the ball tends back toward its rest shape.
In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of easing the deformation.

```motoi
;; A ball that eases into squash and out again
(let ((ball (world/spawn :kind 'circle :x 200 :y 50 :radius 24 :vy 0)))
  (entity/gravity-scale! ball 1.0)
  (entity/bounce! ball 0.8)
  (entity/set! ball 'floor 300)
  (entity/set! ball 'squash-target 1.0)
  (entity/set! ball 'stretch-target 1.0)
  (entity/set! ball 'scale-x 1.0)
  (entity/set! ball 'scale-y 1.0)
  
  (world/after 0 (lambda ()
    (world/each (lambda (e)
      (let* ((ground 300)
             (dist-to-floor (- ground (entity/y e)))
             (target-squash (if (< dist-to-floor 5) 1.5 1.0))
             (target-stretch (if (< dist-to-floor 5) 0.6 1.0))
             (current-x (entity/get e 'scale-x))
             (current-y (entity/get e 'scale-y))
             (ease-rate 0.3)
             (new-x (+ current-x (* (- target-squash current-x) ease-rate)))
             (new-y (+ current-y (* (- target-stretch current-y) ease-rate))))
        (entity/set! e 'scale-x new-x)
        (entity/set! e 'scale-y new-y)))))))
```

The snap is too sudden. Real materials don't instantly change shape — they ease into and out of deformation.

What does this Motoi Scheme program do?

```motoi
;; A ball that eases into squash and out again
(let ((ball (world/spawn :kind 'circle :x 200 :y 50 :radius 24 :vy 0)))
  (entity/gravity-scale! ball 1.0)
  (entity/bounce! ball 0.8)
  (entity/set! ball 'floor 300)
  (entity/set! ball 'squash-target 1.0)
  (entity/set! ball 'stretch-target 1.0)
  (entity/set! ball 'scale-x 1.0)
  (entity/set! ball 'scale-y 1.0)
  
  (world/after 0 (lambda ()
    (world/each (lambda (e)
      (let* ((ground 300)
             (dist-to-floor (- ground (entity/y e)))
             (target-squash (if (< dist-to-floor 5) 1.5 1.0))
             (target-stretch (if (< dist-to-floor 5) 0.6 1.0))
             (current-x (entity/get e 'scale-x))
             (current-y (entity/get e 'scale-y))
             (ease-rate 0.3)
             (new-x (+ current-x (* (- target-squash current-x) ease-rate)))
             (new-y (+ current-y (* (- target-stretch current-y) ease-rate))))
        (entity/set! e 'scale-x new-x)
        (entity/set! e 'scale-y new-y)))))))
```

```motoi
;; A ball that squashes on impact AND stretches when moving fast
(let ((ball (world/spawn :kind 'circle :x 200 :y 50 :radius 24 :vy 0)))
  (entity/gravity-scale! ball 1.0)
  (entity/bounce! ball 0.8)
  (entity/set! ball 'floor 300)
  (entity/set! ball 'scale-x 1.0)
  (entity/set! ball 'scale-y 1.0)

      (let* ((ground 300)
             (dist-to-floor (- ground (entity/y e)))
             (vel-y (cdr (entity/vel e)))
             (speed (abs vel-y))
             (is-on-ground (< dist-to-floor 5))
             
             ;; Impact deformation
             (impact-y (if is-on-ground 0.6 1.0))
             (impact-x (/ 1.0 impact-y))
             
             ;; Velocity deformation
             (velocity-y (+ 1.0 (* speed 0.008)))
             (velocity-x (/ 1.0 velocity-y))
             
             ;; Blend: impact dominates when on ground, velocity otherwise
             (target-y (if is-on-ground impact-y velocity-y))
             (target-x (if is-on-ground impact-x velocity-x))
             
             (current-x (entity/get e 'scale-x))
             (current-y (entity/get e 'scale-y))
             (ease-rate 0.25)
             (new-x (+ current-x (* (- target-x current-x) ease-rate)))
             (new-y (+ current-y (* (- target-y current-y) ease-rate))))
        (entity/set! e 'scale-x new-x)
        (entity/set! e 'scale-y new-y)))))))
```

```motoi
;; A water droplet that wobbles after landing
(let ((drop (world/spawn :kind 'circle :x 200 :y 50 :radius 20 :vy 0)))
  (entity/gravity-scale! drop 1.0)
  (entity/bounce! drop 0.6)
  (entity/set! drop 'floor 300)
  (entity/set! drop 'scale-x 1.0)
  (entity/set! drop 'scale-y 1.0)
  (entity/set! drop 'wobble-phase 0.0)
  (entity/set! drop 'wobble-energy 0.0)
  
  (world/after 0 (lambda ()
    (world/each (lambda (e)
      (let* ((ground 300)
             (dist-to-floor (- ground (entity/y e)))
             (vel-y (cdr (entity/vel e)))
             (speed (abs vel-y))
             (is-on-ground (< dist-to-floor 5))
             
             (wobble-phase (entity/get e 'wobble-phase))
             (wobble-energy (entity/get e 'wobble-energy))
             
             ;; Add energy on impact
             (new-energy (if is-on-ground
                             (+ wobble-energy (* speed 0.02))
                             (* wobble-energy 0.95)))
             
             ;; Advance wobble phase
             (new-phase (+ wobble-phase (* new-energy 0.3)))
             
             ;; Wobble oscillates scale
             (wobble-offset (* (dsin new-phase) new-energy))
             (target-y (+ 1.0 wobble-offset))
             (target-x (/ 1.0 target-y))
             
             (current-x (entity/get e 'scale-x))
             (current-y (entity/get e 'scale-y))
             (ease-rate 0.25)
             (new-x (+ current-x (* (- target-x current-x) ease-rate)))
             (new-y (+ current-y (* (- target-y current-y) ease-rate))))
        
        (entity/set! e 'wobble-phase new-phase)
        (entity/set! e 'wobble-energy new-energy)
        (entity/set! e 'scale-x new-x)
        (entity/set! e 'scale-y new-y)))))))
```

In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of the settle: post-impact overshoot.

```motoi
;; A ball that overshoots its rest shape after squashing
(let ((ball (world/spawn :kind 'circle :x 200 :y 50 :radius 24 :vy 0)))
  (entity/gravity-scale! ball 1.0)
  (entity/bounce! ball 0.75)
  (entity/set! ball 'floor 300)
  (entity/set! ball 'scale-x 1.0)
  (entity/set! ball 'scale-y 1.0)
  (entity/set! ball 'deform-vel 0.0)
  
  (world/after 0 (lambda ()
    (world/each (lambda (e)
      (let* ((ground 300)
             (dist-to-floor (- ground (entity/y e)))
             (vel-y (cdr (entity/vel e)))
             (speed (abs vel-y))
             (is-on-ground (< dist-to-floor 5))
             
             (deform-vel (entity/get e 'deform-vel))
             (current-y (entity/get e 'scale-y))
             
             ;; Push toward squash on impact
             (impact-force (if is-on-ground (* speed -0.02) 0.0))
             
             ;; Spring force toward rest (1.0)
             (spring-force (* (- 1.0 current-y) 0.15))
             
             ;; Damping
             (damping (* deform-vel -0.1))
             
             ;; Update velocity and scale
             (new-vel (+ deform-vel impact-force spring-force damping))
             (new-y (+ current-y new-vel))
             (new-x (/ 1.0 new-y))
             
             (final-x (+ (entity/get e 'scale-x) (* (- new-x (entity/get e 'scale-x)) 0.3)))
             (final-y (+ current-y (* (- new-y current-y) 0.3))))
        
        (entity/set! e 'deform-vel new-vel)
        (entity/set! e 'scale-x final-x)
        (entity/set! e 'scale-y final-y)))))))
```

When a soft object lands, it doesn't just squash and return to rest — it *overshoots*, stretching tall before settling. This overshoot gives the bounce a sense of elasticity.
In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of directional stretch: following the velocity vector.

```motoi
;; A ball that stretches along its velocity vector
(let ((ball (world/spawn :kind 'circle :x 100 :y 50 :radius 22 :vx 8 :vy 0)))
  (entity/gravity-scale! ball 1.0)
  (entity/bounce! ball 0.7)
  (entity/set! ball 'floor 300)
  (entity/set! ball 'scale-x 1.0)
  (entity/set! ball 'scale-y 1.0)
  (entity/set! ball 'rotation 0.0)
  
  (world/after 0 (lambda ()
    (world/each (lambda (e)
      (let* ((vel (entity/vel e))
             (vx (car vel))
             (vy (cdr vel))
             (speed (dsqrt (+ (* vx vx) (* vy vy))))
             (angle (datan2 vy vx))
             
             ;; Stretch along velocity
             (stretch-y (+ 1.0 (* speed 0.008)))
             (stretch-x (/ 1.0 stretch-y))
             
             (current-x (entity/get e 'scale-x))
             (current-y (entity/get e 'scale-y))
             (current-rot (entity/get e 'rotation))
             (ease-rate 0.2)
             (new-x (+ current-x (* (- stretch-x current-x) ease-rate)))
             (new-y (+ current-y (* (- stretch-y current-y) ease-rate)))
             (new-rot (+ current-rot (* (- angle current-rot) ease-rate))))
        
        (entity/set! e 'scale-x new-x)
        (entity/set! e 'scale-y new-y)
        (entity/set! e 'rotation new-rot)))))))
```

```motoi
  (world/after 0 (lambda ()
    (world/each (lambda (e)
      (let* ((vel (entity/vel e))
             (vx (car vel))
             (vy (cdr vel))
             (speed (dsqrt (+ (* vx vx) (* vy vy))))
             (angle (datan2 vy vx))
             
             ;; Stretch along velocity
             (stretch-y (+ 1.0 (* speed 0.008)))
             (stretch-x (/ 1.0 stretch-y))
             
             (current-x (entity/get e 'scale-x))
             (current-y (entity/get e 'scale-y))
             (current-rot (entity/get e 'rotation))
             (ease-rate 0.2)
             (new-x (+ current-x (* (- stretch-x current-x) ease-rate)))
             (new-y (+ current-y (* (- stretch-y current-y) ease-rate)))
             (new-rot (+ current-rot (* (- angle current-rot) ease-rate))))
        
        (entity/set! e 'scale-x new-x)
        (entity/set! e 'scale-y new-y)
        (entity/set! e 'rotation new-rot)))))))
```
So far, we've only stretched along the vertical axis. A ball moving diagonally should stretch along its direction of travel.

```motoi
;; A ball that squashes before jumping
(let ((ball (world/spawn :kind 'circle :x 200 :y 280 :radius 24 :vy 0)))
  (entity/set! ball 'floor 300)
  (entity/set! ball 'scale-x 1.0)
  (entity/set! ball 'scale-y 1.0)

  (entity/set! ball 'timer 0)
  
  (world/after 0 (lambda ()

      (let* ((phase (entity/get e 'phase))
             (timer (entity/get e 'timer))
             (new-timer (+ timer 1)))
        
        (cond
          ((eq? phase 'anticipate)
           (let* ((progress (/ timer 20.0))
                  (squash (+ 1.0 (* progress -0.5)))
                  (target-y squash)
                  (target-x (/ 1.0 target-y)))
             (entity/set! e 'scale-x target-x)
             (entity/set! e 'scale-y target-y)
             (when (> timer 20)
               (entity/set! e 'phase 'launch)
               (entity/set! e 'timer 0)
               (entity/set-vel! e 0 -15)
               (entity/gravity-scale! e 1.0)
               (entity/bounce! e 0.7))))
          
          ((eq? phase 'launch)
           (let* ((vel-y (cdr (entity/vel e)))
                  (speed (abs vel-y))
                  (target-y (+ 1.0 (* speed 0.01)))
                  (target-x (/ 1.0 target-y))
                  (current-x (entity/get e 'scale-x))
                  (current-y (entity/get e 'scale-y))
                  (ease-rate 0.2)
                  (new-x (+ current-x (* (- target-x current-x) ease-rate)))
                  (new-y (+ current-y (* (- target-y current-y) ease-rate))))
             (entity/set! e 'scale-x new-x)
             (entity/set! e 'scale-y new-y))))
        
        (entity/set! e 'timer new-timer)))))))
```

```motoi
;; A blinking eye
(let ((eye (world/spawn :kind 'circle :x 200 :y 200 :radius 20)))
  (entity/set! eye 'scale-x 1.0)
  (entity/set! eye 'scale-y 1.0)
  (entity/set! eye 'timer 0)

          ((eq? phase 'opening)
           (let* ((progress (/ timer 6.0))
                  (scale-y (+ 0.15 (* progress 0.85))))
             (entity/set! e 'scale-y scale-y)
             (entity/set! e 'scale-x 1.0)
             (when (>= timer 6)
               (entity/set! e 'phase 'open)
               (entity/set! e 'timer 0)))))
        
        (entity/set! e 'timer new-timer)))))))
```

In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of squash-stretch dialogue: action and reaction.

```motoi
;; Two balls colliding with reciprocal deformation
(let ((ball-a (world/spawn :kind 'circle :x 150 :y 200 :radius 24 :vx 6 :vy 0))
      (ball-b (world/spawn :kind 'circle :x 350 :y 200 :radius 24 :vx -4 :vy 0)))
  
  (entity/set! ball-a 'scale-x 1.0)
  (entity/set! ball-a 'scale-y 1.0)
  (entity/set! ball-b 'scale-x 1.0)
  (entity/set! ball-b 'scale-y 1.0)
  (entity/set! ball-a 'name 'a)
  (entity/set! ball-b 'name 'b)
  
  (world/after 0 (lambda ()
    (let* ((a (world/find (lambda (e) (eq? (entity/get e 'name) 'a))))
           (b (world/find (lambda (e) (eq? (entity/get e 'name) 'b))))
           (dist (entity/distance a b))
           (colliding (< dist 48)))
      
      (if colliding
          (let* ((vel-a (entity/vel a))
                 (vel-b (entity/vel b))
                 (rel-vel (- (car vel-a) (car vel-b)))
                 (impact-force (abs rel-vel))
                 (squash-a (- 1.0 (* impact-force 0.05)))
                 (squash-b (- 1.0 (* impact-force 0.05))))
            (entity/set! a 'scale-x (/ 1.0 squash-a))
            (entity/set! a 'scale-y squash-a)
            (entity/set! b 'scale-x (/ 1.0 squash-b))
            (entity/set! b 'scale-y squash-b)
            (entity/set-vel! a (* (car vel-a) -0.8) 0)
            (entity/set-vel! b (* (car vel-b) -0.8) 0))
          
          (let* ((current-a-x (entity/get a 'scale-x))
                 (current-a-y (entity/get a 'scale-y))
                 (current-b-x (entity/get b 'scale-x))
                 (current-b-y (entity/get b 'scale-y))
                 (ease-rate 0.2)
                 (new-a-x (+ current-a-x (* (- 1.0 current-a-x) ease-rate)))
                 (new-a-y (+ current-a-y (* (- 1.0 current-a-y) ease-rate)))
                 (new-b-x (+ current-b-x (* (- 1.0 current-b-x) ease-rate)))
                 (new-b-y (+ current-b-y (* (- 1.0 current-b-y) ease-rate))))
            (entity/set! a 'scale-x new-a-x)
            (entity/set! a 'scale-y new-a-y)
            (entity/set! b 'scale-x new-b-x)
            (entity/set! b 'scale-y new-b-y)))))))
```

```motoi
;; A spring compressed and released
(let ((spring (world/spawn :kind 'circle :x 200 :y 200 :radius 20)))
  (entity/set! spring 'scale-x 1.0)
  (entity/set! spring 'scale-y 1.0)
  (entity/set! spring 'compress-vel 0.0)
  (entity/set! spring 'timer 0)
  (entity/set! spring 'phase 'idle)
  
  (world/after 0 (lambda ()
    (world/each (lambda (e)
      (let* ((phase (entity/get e 'phase))
             (timer (entity/get e 'timer))
             (new-timer (+ timer 1)))
        
        (cond
          ((eq? phase 'idle)
           (when (> timer 30)
             (entity/set! e 'phase 'compress)
             (entity/set! e 'timer 0)))
          
          ((eq? phase 'compress)
           (let* ((progress (/ timer 10.0))
                  (scale-y (- 1.0 (* progress 0.6))))
             (entity/set! e 'scale-y scale-y)
             (entity/set! e 'scale-x (/ 1.0 scale-y))
             (when (>= timer 10)
               (entity/set! e 'phase 'release)
               (entity/set! e 'compress-vel 0.1)
               (entity/set! e 'timer 0))))
          
          ((eq? phase 'release)
           (let* ((compress-vel (entity/get e 'compress-vel))
                  (current-y (entity/get e 'scale-y))
                  (spring-force (* (- 1.0 current-y) 0.2))
                  (damping (* compress-vel -0.08))
                  (new-vel (+ compress-vel spring-force damping))
                  (new-y (+ current-y new-vel))
                  (new-x (/ 1.0 new-y)))
             (entity/set! e 'compress-vel new-vel)
             (entity/set! e 'scale-y new-y)
             (entity/set! e 'scale-x new-x))))
        
        (entity/set! e 'timer new-timer)))))))
```

```motoi
  (world/after 0 (lambda ()
    (world/each (lambda (e)
```
```motoi
      (let* ((timer (entity/get e 'timer))
             (new-timer (+ timer 1))
             (inflate-vel (entity/get e 'inflate-vel))
             (current-scale (entity/get e 'scale-x))
             (max-scale 2.5)
             (inflate-force (if (< current-scale max-scale) 0.015 0.0))
             (drag (* inflate-vel -0.05))
             (new-vel (+ inflate-vel inflate-force drag))
             (new-scale (+ current-scale new-vel)))
        
        (entity/set! e 'inflate-vel new-vel)
        (entity/set! e 'scale-x new-scale)
        (entity/set! e 'scale-y new-scale)
        (entity/set! e 'timer new-timer)))))))
```

In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of the pop: sudden expansion.

```motoi
;; A balloon popping
(let ((balloon (world/spawn :kind 'circle :x 200 :y 200 :radius 20)))
  (entity/set! balloon 'scale-x 1.0)
  (entity/set! balloon 'scale-y 1.0)
  (entity/set! balloon 'opacity 1.0)
  (entity/set! balloon 'timer 0)
  (entity/set! balloon 'phase 'idle)
  
  (world/after 0 (lambda ()
    (world/each (lambda (e)
      (let* ((phase (entity/get e 'phase))
             (timer (entity/get e 'timer))
             (new-timer (+ timer 1)))
        
        (cond
          ((eq? phase 'idle)
           (when (> timer 40)
             (entity/set! e 'phase 'pop)
             (entity/set! e 'timer 0)))
          
          ((eq? phase 'pop)
           (let* ((progress (/ timer 8.0))
                  (scale (+ 1.0 (* progress 2.5)))
                  (opacity (- 1.0 (* progress 1.2))))
             (entity/set! e 'scale-x scale)
             (entity/set! e 'scale-y scale)
             (entity/set! e 'opacity opacity)
             (when (>= timer 8)
               (entity/despawn! e)))))
        
        (entity/set! e 'timer new-timer)))))))
```

In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of stretch leading, squash following: the wave.

```motoi
;; A worm with a traveling wave of squash-stretch
(let ((head (world/spawn :kind 'circle :x 150 :y 200 :radius 18))
      (mid (world/spawn :kind 'circle :x 200 :y 200 :radius 18))
      (tail (world/spawn :kind 'circle :x 250 :y 200 :radius 18)))
  
  (entity/set! head 'part 'head)
  (entity/set! mid 'part 'mid)
  (entity/set! tail 'part 'tail)
  (entity/set! head 'scale-x 1.0)
  (entity/set! head 'scale-y 1.0)
  (entity/set! mid 'scale-x 1.0)
  (entity/set! mid 'scale-y 1.0)
  (entity/set! tail 'scale-x 1.0)
  (entity/set! tail 'scale-y 1.0)
  (entity/set! head 'phase 0.0)
  
  (world/after 0 (lambda ()
    (let* ((head-entity (world/find (lambda (e) (eq? (entity/get e 'part) 'head))))
           (phase (entity/get head-entity 'phase))
           (new-phase (+ phase 0.15)))
      
      (world/each (lambda (e)
        (let* ((part (entity/get e 'part))
               (offset (cond
                         ((eq? part 'head) 0.0)
                         ((eq? part 'mid) 1.5)
                         ((eq? part 'tail) 3.0)
                         (else 0.0)))
               (local-phase (+ new-phase offset))
               (wave (dsin local-phase))
               (scale-x (+ 1.0 (* wave 0.3)))
               (scale-y (/ 1.0 scale-x)))
          (entity/set! e 'scale-x scale-x)
          (entity/set! e 'scale-y scale-y))))
      
      (entity/set! head-entity 'phase new-phase)))))
```

In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of recoil: stretch before squash.

```motoi
;; An arm recoiling before punching
(let ((fist (world/spawn :kind 'circle :x 150 :y 200 :radius 18))
      (arm (world/spawn :kind 'circle :x 200 :y 200 :radius 14)))
  
  (entity/set! fist 'part 'fist)
  (entity/set! arm 'part 'arm)
  (entity/set! fist 'scale-x 1.0)
  (entity/set! fist 'scale-y 1.0)
  (entity/set! arm 'scale-x 1.0)
  (entity/set! arm 'scale-y 1.0)
  (entity/set! fist 'timer 0)
  (entity/set! fist 'phase 'recoil)
  
  (world/after 0 (lambda ()
    (let* ((fist-entity (world/find (lambda (e) (eq? (entity/get e 'part) 'fist))))
           (phase (entity/get fist-entity 'phase))
           (timer (entity/get fist-entity 'timer))
           (new-timer (+ timer 1)))
      
      (cond
        ((eq? phase 'recoil)
         (let* ((progress (/ timer 15.0))
                (stretch-x (+ 1.0 (* progress 0.4)))
                (stretch-y (/ 1.0 stretch-x)))
           (entity/set! fist-entity 'scale-x stretch-x)
           (entity/set! fist-entity 'scale-y stretch-y)
           (when (>= timer 15)
             (entity/set! fist-entity 'phase 'strike)
             (entity/set! fist-entity 'timer 0))))
        
        ((eq? phase 'strike)
         (let* ((progress (/ timer 5.0))
                (squash-x (- 1.4 (* progress 0.4)))
                (squash-y (/ 1.0 squash-x)))
           (entity/set! fist-entity 'scale-x squash-x)
           (entity/set! fist-entity 'scale-y squash-y)
           (entity/move! fist-entity 15 0)
           (when (>= timer 5)
             (entity/set! fist-entity 'phase 'recover)
             (entity/set! fist-entity 'timer 0))))
        
        ((eq? phase 'recover)
         (let* ((current-x (entity/get fist-entity 'scale-x))
                (current-y (entity/get fist-entity 'scale-y))
                (ease-rate 0.15)
                (new-x (+ current-x (* (- 1.0 current-x) ease-rate)))
                (new-y (+ current-y (* (- 1.0 current-y) ease-rate))))
           (entity/set! fist-entity 'scale-x new-x)
           (entity/set! fist-entity 'scale-y new-y))))
      
      (entity/set! fist-entity 'timer new-timer)))))
```

A punch or strike often *stretches* the arm back before releasing forward. This is the opposite of anticipation — the windup is a stretch, not a squash.
How do I work with characters for squash and stretch in Motoi?

```motoi
;; A character skidding to a stop
(let ((body (world/spawn :kind 'circle :x 100 :y 200 :radius 26 :vx 12 :vy 0)))
  (entity/set! body 'scale-x 1.0)
  (entity/set! body 'scale-y 1.0)
  (entity/set! body 'phase 'slide)
  
  (world/after 0 (lambda ()
    (world/each (lambda (e)
      (let* ((phase (entity/get e 'phase))
             (vx (car (entity/vel e))))
        
        (cond
          ((eq? phase 'slide)
           (when (> (entity/x e) 300)
             (entity/set-vel! e 0 0)
             (entity/set! e 'phase 'skid)))
          
          ((eq? phase 'skid)
           (let* ((current-x (entity/get e 'scale-x))
                  (current-y (entity/get e 'scale-y))
                  (target-x 0.6)
                  (target-y (/ 1.0 target-x))
                  (ease-rate 0.15)
                  (new-x (+ current-x (* (- target-x current-x) ease-rate)))
                  (new-y (+ current-y (* (- target-y current-y) ease-rate))))
             (entity/set! e 'scale-x new-x)
             (entity/set! e 'scale-y new-y)
             (when (< (abs (- new-x target-x)) 0.05)
               (entity/set! e 'phase 'recover))))
          
          ((eq? phase 'recover)
           (let* ((current-x (entity/get e 'scale-x))
                  (current-y (entity/get e 'scale-y))
                  (ease-rate 0.1)
                  (new-x (+ current-x (* (- 1.0 current-x) ease-rate)))
                  (new-y (+ current-y (* (- 1.0 current-y) ease-rate))))
             (entity/set! e 'scale-x new-x)
             (entity/set! e 'scale-y new-y))))))))))
```

In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of chain reaction: delayed squash on multiple objects.

```motoi
(call-with-world
 (make-world width: 600 height: 300 background: '(245 245 250))
 (lambda (w)
   (let ((frame 0))
     (define (squash-at-phase p)
       (let ((local-t (modulo p 1.0)))
         (if (< local-t 0.2)
             (- 1.0 (* 0.6 (/ local-t 0.2)))
             (if (< local-t 0.4)
                 (+ 0.4 (* 0.6 (/ (- local-t 0.2) 0.2)))
                 1.0))))
     (define (draw)
       (world-clear w)
       (let* ((t (/ frame 90.0))
              (s1 (squash-at-phase t))
              (s2 (squash-at-phase (- t 0.15)))
              (s3 (squash-at-phase (- t 0.3))))
```

In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of squash with particle emission.

```motoi
(call-with-world
 (make-world width: 400 height: 400 background: '(250 248 245))
 (lambda (w)
   (let ((frame 0)
         (particles '()))
     (define (draw)
       (world-clear w)
       (let* ((t (/ frame 60.0))
              (squash (+ 0.6 (* 0.4 (sin (* t 4.0)))))
              (h-scale (/ 1.0 (sqrt squash)))
              (v-scale (sqrt squash)))
         (when (and (< squash 0.65) (= (modulo frame 15) 0))
           (set! particles (cons (list 200 200 (* 2.0 (- (random) 0.5)) (* 2.0 (- (random) 0.5)) 30)
                                 particles)))
         (set! particles
               (filter (lambda (p)
                         (let ((ttl (list-ref p 4)))
                           (> ttl 0)))
                       (map (lambda (p)
                              (list (+ (car p) (caddr p))
                                    (+ (cadr p) (cadddr p))
                                    (caddr p)
                                    (cadddr p)
                                    (- (list-ref p 4) 1)))
                            particles)))
         (for-each (lambda (p)
```

```motoi
                                    (caddr p)
```

```motoi
(let ((e (world/spawn 'weight 320 240))
      (pos 240)
      (vel 0))
  (entity/sprite! e "⚓")

                   (let* ((target 240)
                          (spring-force (* -0.02 (- pos target)))
                          (damping (* -0.1 vel)))
```

In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of coupled oscillators.

```motoi
(let ((a (world/spawn 'a 280 240))
      (b (world/spawn 'b 360 240))
      (pos-a 240)
      (pos-b 240)
      (vel-a 0)
      (vel-b 0))
  (entity/sprite! a "🔴")
  (entity/sprite! b "🔵")
  (letrec ((loop (lambda (t)
                   (let* ((spring-a (* -0.03 (- pos-a 240)))
                          (spring-b (* -0.03 (- pos-b 240)))
                          (coupling (* 0.005 (- pos-b pos-a)))
                          (damping-a (* -0.05 vel-a))
                          (damping-b (* -0.05 vel-b)))
                     (set! vel-a (+ vel-a spring-a coupling damping-a))
                     (set! vel-b (+ vel-b spring-b (- coupling) damping-b))
                     (set! pos-a (+ pos-a vel-a))
                     (set! pos-b (+ pos-b vel-b))
                     (entity/set-pos! a 280 pos-a)
                     (entity/set-pos! b 360 pos-b))
                   (world/step)
                   (when (< t 600)
                     (loop (+ t 1))))))
    (set! pos-a 180)
    (set! pos-b 300)
    (loop 0)))
```

How do I work with lists for wave interference (two sources) in Motoi?

```motoi
(let ((source-a (list 250 240))
      (source-b (list 390 240))
      (probe (world/spawn 'probe 320 240)))
  (entity/sprite! probe "⚪")
  (letrec ((loop (lambda (t)
                   (let* ((dist-a (dhypot (- 320 (car source-a)) (- 240 (cadr source-a))))
                          (dist-b (dhypot (- 320 (car source-b)) (- 240 (cadr source-b))))
                          (wave-a (dsin (- (* 0.1 t) (* 0.05 dist-a))))
                          (wave-b (dsin (- (* 0.1 t) (* 0.05 dist-b))))
                          (interference (+ wave-a wave-b))
                          (brightness (+ 0.5 (* 0.5 interference))))
                     (entity/set! probe 'opacity brightness))
                   (world/step)
                   (when (< t 600)
                     (loop (+ t 1))))))
    (loop 0)))
```

How do I work with lists for gravity: the constant pull in Motoi?

```motoi
;; A single entity falls under gravity.
(world/reset!)
(world/gravity! 0 -0.3)  ; downward pull, 0.3 px/frame²
(world/floor! 50)        ; invisible floor at y=50

(define circle
  (world/spawn 'circle 200 300 :radius 12 :color 'skyblue))

(world/step 120)
```

Gravity is the simplest force: a fixed acceleration applied to every entity, every frame, in a chosen direction. By default, `world/gravity!` pulls downward (negative Y), but you can point it anywhere.

```motoi
;; Wind that oscillates east-west.

(world/gravity! 0 -0.1)

  (world/spawn 'circle 200 250 :radius 8 :color 'cyan))

      (loop (+ frame 1)))))
```

```motoi
;; Impulse toward a target point.
(world/reset!)

(define target-x 300)
(define target-y 200)

(define agent
  (world/spawn 'circle 100 250 :radius 8 :color 'lime))
(entity/set-vel! agent 0.5 -0.3)  ; initial drift
(entity/drag! agent 0.97)

(world/step 40)  ; drift a bit

(let* ((px (entity/x agent))
       (py (entity/y agent))
       (dx (- target-x px))
       (dy (- target-y py))
       (dist (dsqrt (+ (* dx dx) (* dy dy))))
       (nx (/ dx dist))
       (ny (/ dy dist)))
  (world/impulse! agent (* nx 3.0) (* ny 3.0)))

(world/step 100)
```

In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of wind affecting only certain entities: selective forces.

```motoi
;; Wind affects only "light" entities.
(world/reset!)
(world/gravity! 0 -0.15)
(world/wind! 0.2 0)
(world/floor! 60)

(define entities
  (map (lambda (i)
         (let ((e (world/spawn 'circle (+ 100 (* i 30)) 250 :radius 6
                               :color (if (< i 3) 'cyan 'brown))))
           (entity/set! e 'type (if (< i 3) 'light 'heavy))
           (if (< i 3)
               (begin
                 (entity/drag! e 0.94)
                 (entity/gravity-scale! e 0.5))
               (entity/mass! e 8.0))
           e))
       (list 0 1 2 3 4 5)))

;; Override wind per-entity by checking tag (manual step loop):
(let loop ((frame 0))
  (when (< frame 150)
    (world/each
      (lambda (e)
        (when (eq? (entity/get e 'type) 'light)
          ;; Light entities feel wind via manual accel (or let world/wind! do it)
          (void))))
    (world/step 1)
    (loop (+ frame 1))))
```

```motoi
  (map (lambda (i)
         (let ((e (world/spawn 'circle (+ 100 (* i 30)) 250 :radius 6
                               :color (if (< i 3) 'cyan 'brown))))
           (entity/set! e 'type (if (< i 3) 'light 'heavy))
           (if (< i 3)
               (begin
                 (entity/drag! e 0.94)
                 (entity/gravity-scale! e 0.5))
               (entity/mass! e 8.0))
           e))
       (list 0 1 2 3 4 5)))

;; Override wind per-entity by checking tag (manual step loop):
(let loop ((frame 0))
  (when (< frame 150)
    (world/each
      (lambda (e)
        (when (eq? (entity/get e 'type) 'light)
          ;; Light entities feel wind via manual accel (or let world/wind! do it)
          (void))))
    (world/step 1)
    (loop (+ frame 1))))
```
Not every entity needs to feel every force. Use `entity/get` tags or position checks to apply wind only to a subset.
In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of attract + impulse combo: catch and release.

```motoi
;; Catch and release.
(world/reset!)

(define particle
  (world/spawn 'circle 320 100 :radius 6 :color 'violet))
(entity/set-vel! particle -1.2 0.8)
(entity/drag! particle 0.98)

(let loop ((frame 0))
  (when (< frame 200)
    (world/attract! 200 200 70 2)
    (world/step 1)
    ;; Check distance; if close, impulse away
    (let* ((dx (- (entity/x particle) 200))
           (dy (- (entity/y particle) 200))
           (dist (dsqrt (+ (* dx dx) (* dy dy)))))
      (when (< dist 30)
        (let ((nx (/ dx dist))
              (ny (/ dy dist)))
          (world/impulse! particle (* nx 5.0) (* ny 5.0)))))
    (loop (+ frame 1))))
```

How do I work with combining forces with easing (aspirational) in Motoi?

```motoi
;; Attract strength eases in.
(world/reset!)

(define particle
  (world/spawn 'circle 100 200 :radius 6 :color 'pink))
(entity/set-vel! particle 1.5 0)
(entity/drag! particle 0.98)

(let loop ((frame 0))
  (when (< frame 180)
    (let ((t (/ (min frame 60.0) 60.0))
          (strength (* 80 (* t t))))  ; quadratic ease-in
      (world/attract! 250 200 strength 2)
      (world/step 1)
      (loop (+ frame 1)))))
```

Forces generate *velocity*, not *position*. Easing is a position-over-time curve.

```motoi
(define (update t)
```
```motoi
  (letrec
```
```motoi
    ((step (lambda (x y vx vy trail frame)
             (if (> frame 300)
                 '()
                 (let* ((gravity 0.3)
                        (new-trail (if (= (modulo frame 20) 0)
                                       (cons (list x y) trail)
                                       trail))
                        (pull (lambda (tx ty)
                                (let* ((dx (- tx x)) (dy (- ty y))
                                       (dist (sqrt (+ (* dx dx) (* dy dy)))))
                                  (if (< dist 50)
                                      (list (* 0.01 (/ dx dist)) (* 0.01 (/ dy dist)))
                                      (list 0 0)))))
                        (forces (map (lambda (p) (pull (car p) (cadr p))) new-trail))
                        (sum-fx (apply + (map car forces)))
                        (sum-fy (apply + (map cadr forces))))
                   (cons
                     (draw-circle x y 8 "purple")
                     (step (+ x vx) (+ y vy)
                           (+ vx sum-fx) (+ vy sum-fy gravity)
                           new-trail (+ frame 1))))))))
    (step 50 100 2.0 0 '() 0)))
```

```motoi
(let ((walker (world/spawn 40 180)))
  (entity/sprite! walker 'stick-figure)
```
```motoi
  (letrec ((step-cycle (lambda (t)
```
```motoi
                         (let* ((bob-y (* 4 (dsin (* t PI))))
                                (squash-phase (dcos (* t PI)))
                                (squash-y (* 2 (max 0 squash-phase))))
                           (entity/set-pos! walker 
                             (entity/x walker) 
                             (+ 180 bob-y squash-y))
                           (part/step walker 
                             (if (< (modulo t 2) 1) :left :right)

                           (world/after 0.4 (lambda () (step-cycle (+ t 1))))))))

  (world/step 120))
```

In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of arm swing.

```motoi
(let ((walker (world/spawn 40 180)))
  (entity/sprite! walker 'stick-figure)
  (entity/set-vel! walker 1.5 0)
  (letrec ((step-cycle (lambda (t)
                         (let ((bob-y (* 3 (dsin (* t PI))))
                               (left-leg? (< (modulo t 2) 1))
                               (arm-phase (dsin (* (+ t 1) PI))))
                           (entity/set-pos! walker 
                             (entity/x walker) 
                             (+ 180 bob-y))
                           (part/step walker 
                             (if left-leg? :left :right) 
                             :duration 0.4)
                           ;; swing opposite arm
                           (part/raise walker 
                             (if left-leg? :right-arm :left-arm) 
                             :by (* 8 arm-phase) 
                             :duration 0.4)
                           (world/after 0.4 (lambda () (step-cycle (+ t 1))))))))
    (step-cycle 0))
  (world/step 120))
```

Arms counterbalance legs. When the left leg swings forward, the right arm swings forward.

```motoi
(let ((walker (world/spawn 160 180)))

  (motion/march walker :vel 1.2 :cadence 0.4)

    (part/turn walker :by (/ PI 2) :duration 1.0)
    (entity/set-vel! walker 0 -1.2)))
```

How do I use `let` for walk to a target in Motoi?

```motoi
(let ((walker (world/spawn 40 180))
      (target (world/spawn 280 80)))
  (entity/sprite! walker 'stick-figure)
  (entity/sprite! target 'flag)
  (let* ((dx (- (entity/x target) (entity/x walker)))
         (dy (- (entity/y target) (entity/y walker)))
         (dist (dsqrt (+ (* dx dx) (* dy dy))))
         (speed 1.5)
         (vx (* speed (/ dx dist)))
         (vy (* speed (/ dy dist))))
    (entity/set-vel! walker vx vy)
    (motion/march walker :vel speed :cadence 0.4)
    (part/turn walker :to (datan2 dy dx) :duration 0.2))
  (world/step 180))
```

Often a character needs to walk *toward* something. We calculate the direction, set the velocity, and let the step cycle run.

```motoi
(let ((walker (world/spawn 40 180))
```
```motoi
      (mark (world/spawn 200 180)))
  (entity/sprite! walker 'stick-figure)
  (entity/sprite! mark 'x-mark)
  (let* ((dist (- (entity/x mark) (entity/x walker)))
         (speed 1.5)
         (time (/ dist speed)))
    (entity/set-vel! walker speed 0)
    (motion/march walker :vel speed :cadence 0.4)
```

In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of skipping.

```motoi
(let ((skipper (world/spawn 40 180)))
  (entity/sprite! skipper 'stick-figure)
  (letrec ((skip-cycle (lambda (t)
                         (let* ((hop? (< (modulo t 2) 1))
                                (bob-depth (if hop? 12 4))
                                (airtime (if hop? 0.15 0.0)))
                           (entity/set-vel! skipper 1.8 0)
                           (let ((bob-y (* bob-depth (dsin (* t PI)))))
                             (entity/set-pos! skipper 
                               (entity/x skipper) 
                               (+ 180 bob-y)))
                           (part/step skipper 
                             (if (< (modulo t 2) 1) :left :right) 
                             :duration 0.35 
                             :airtime airtime)
                           (world/after 0.35 (lambda () (skip-cycle (+ t 1))))))))
    (skip-cycle 0))
  (world/step 150))
```

```motoi

      (hesitant (world/spawn 40 200)))

  (entity/sprite! hesitant 'stick-figure)
  
  ;; confident: anticipation, crisp timing, strong settle
  (motion/march confident 
    :vel 1.6

    :style :confident)
  
  ;; hesitant: follow-through, slow settle, short steps
  (motion/march hesitant 
    :vel 0.9 
    :cadence 0.5 
    :style :hesitant)
  
  (world/step 150))
```

How do I work with characters for putting it together: a journey in Motoi?

```motoi
(let ((traveler (world/spawn 40 200))
      (fork (world/spawn 160 200))
      (hill-base (world/spawn 240 200))
      (summit (world/spawn 280 120)))
  (entity/sprite! traveler 'stick-figure)
  (entity/sprite! fork 'signpost)
  (entity/sprite! summit 'flag)
  
  ;; walk to fork
  (motion/march traveler :vel 1.5 :cadence 0.4)
  
  ;; pause and choose
  (world/after 2.5 (lambda ()
    (motion/halt traveler :over 0.3)
    (world/after 1.0 (lambda ()
      ;; turn toward hill
      (part/turn traveler :by (/ PI -4) :duration 0.5)
      (motion/glide traveler :to-vel 1.2 -0.5 :over 0.5)
      (motion/march traveler :vel 1.3 :cadence 0.45 :lean (/ PI 12))
      
      ;; arrive at summit
      (world/after 4.0 (lambda ()
        (motion/halt traveler :over 0.5)
        (part/expression traveler :proud :duration 1.0)))))))
  
  (world/step 480))
```

In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of contact and flight.

```motoi
(motion
  (actor 'jogger
    (shape 'circle 20)
    (color 220 80 60))
  (on-tick
    (lambda (t)
      (let* ((phase (modulo t 40))
             (x (+ 150 (* t 3)))
             (flight? (and (> phase 15) (< phase 25)))
             (y (if flight?
                    (- 280 (* 12 (sin (* (- phase 15) (/ 3.14159 10)))))
                    290)))
```

How do I work with lists for path following in Motoi?

```motoi
(motion
  (actor 'follower
    (shape 'circle 18)
    (color 200 120 60))
  (on-tick
    (lambda (t)
      (let* ((waypoints '((100 . 100) (200 . 150) (300 . 120) (400 . 200)))
             (segment-duration 60)
             (total-duration (* segment-duration (- (length waypoints) 1)))
             (clamped-t (modulo t total-duration))
             (segment (quotient clamped-t segment-duration))
             (local-t (modulo clamped-t segment-duration))
             (progress (/ local-t segment-duration))
             (p0 (list-ref waypoints segment))
             (p1 (list-ref waypoints (+ segment 1)))
             (x (+ (car p0) (* progress (- (car p1) (car p0)))))
             (y (+ (cdr p0) (* progress (- (cdr p1) (cdr p0))))))
        (entity 'follower (at x y))))))
```

In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of variable speed along path.

```motoi
(motion
  (actor 'racer
    (shape 'circle 18)
    (color 180 60 60))
  (on-tick
    (lambda (t)
      (let* ((waypoints '((100 . 100) (200 . 150) (300 . 120) (400 . 200)))
             (segment-duration-base 60)
             (segment (modulo (quotient t segment-duration-base) (- (length waypoints) 1)))
             (local-t (modulo t segment-duration-base))
             (corner? (or (= segment 1) (= segment 2)))
             (speed-mult (if corner? 0.6 1.2))
             (adjusted-duration (/ segment-duration-base speed-mult))
             (progress (/ local-t adjusted-duration))
             (clamped-progress (min 1.0 progress))
             (p0 (list-ref waypoints segment))
             (p1 (list-ref waypoints (+ segment 1)))
             (x (+ (car p0) (* clamped-progress (- (car p1) (car p0)))))
             (y (+ (cdr p0) (* clamped-progress (- (cdr p1) (cdr p0))))))
        (entity 'racer (at x y))))))
```

In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of gaze tracking: following a moving target.

```motoi
(let ((head (world/spawn 160 100 :sprite "circle" :color "tan"))
      (bird (world/spawn 50 80 :sprite "circle" :size [6 6] :color "blue")))
```

In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of reach with lead: intercepting a moving target.

```motoi
(let ((arm (world/spawn 140 130 :sprite "line" :length 40 :color "peach"))
      (ball (world/spawn 200 120 :sprite "circle" :size [8 8] :color "orange")))
  (entity/set-vel! ball 1.5 -0.5)
  (let* ((bx (entity/x ball))
         (by (entity/y ball))
         (bvx (car (entity/vel ball)))
         (bvy (cadr (entity/vel ball)))
         (reach-duration 50)
         (lead-x (+ bx (* bvx reach-duration)))
         (lead-y (+ by (* bvy reach-duration))))
    (part/reach arm :target-x lead-x :target-y lead-y :duration reach-duration)))

(world/step 70)
```

In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of the simplest secondary: a leader and a follower.

```motoi
(let* ((leader (world/spawn 100 150))
       (follower (world/spawn 100 150)))
  (entity/sprite! leader "circle" 12 "blue")
  (entity/sprite! follower "circle" 8 "cyan")
  
  ;; leader moves in a slow arc
  (motion/arc leader 300 150 60 120)
  
  ;; follower tries to catch up every frame
  (world/after 1
    (lambda ()
      (let* ((lx (entity/x leader))
             (ly (entity/y leader))
             (fx (entity/x follower))
             (fy (entity/y follower))
             (dx (- lx fx))
             (dy (- ly fy)))
        ;; move 10% of the way toward leader each frame
        (entity/move! follower (* dx 0.1) (* dy 0.1))
        (when (world/alive? leader)
          (world/after 1 (world/current-callback)))))))
```

Show me a recursive example of secondary motion in Motoi.

```motoi
(let* ((head (world/spawn 100 150))
       (tail-1 (world/spawn 100 150))
       (tail-2 (world/spawn 100 150))
       (tail-3 (world/spawn 100 150))
       (t 0))
  (entity/sprite! head "circle" 14 "darkgreen")
  (entity/sprite! tail-1 "circle" 11 "green")
  (entity/sprite! tail-2 "circle" 9 "limegreen")
  (entity/sprite! tail-3 "circle" 7 "palegreen")
  
  ;; head moves in a figure-eight pattern
  (world/after 1
    (lambda ()
      (set! t (+ t 0.05))
      (let ((x (+ 200 (* 80 (dsin t))))
            (y (+ 150 (* 60 (dsin (* t 2))))))
        (entity/set-pos! head x y))
      
      ;; tail segments chase with varying lag rates
      (let ((chase (lambda (follower leader rate)
                     (let* ((lx (entity/x leader))
                            (ly (entity/y leader))
                            (fx (entity/x follower))
                            (fy (entity/y follower))
                            (dx (- lx fx))
                            (dy (- ly fy)))
                       (entity/move! follower (* dx rate) (* dy rate))))))
        (chase tail-1 head 0.15)
        (chase tail-2 tail-1 0.15)
        (chase tail-3 tail-2 0.15))
      
      (world/after 1 (world/current-callback)))))
```
